what: Probe β piece 3c — CPU construction search (stochastic greedy + penalty/swap local search), the witness round's unanimous pivot, prototyped
verdict: RED — and notably WEAKER than CP-SAT. Naive unstructured construction search plateaus far below records.
key_datapoint: A(14,6,6): construction found 25 vs CP-SAT 30 vs best_known 42 — heuristic search is WORSE than exact search here, and both are far below the structured record.
validity: prototype is correct (reaches known optima 4/7/3 on small cells A(6,4,3)/A(7,4,3)/A(9,6,4); all outputs verify) — so the RED on large cells is a real plateau, not a bug.
reading: Unstructured search — exact (CP-SAT) OR heuristic (local search) — plateaus far below records; the records require STRUCTURE (automorphism-prescribed / algebraic / learned-construction a la FunSearch). This KILLS the 'naive massively-parallel construction' sleeper (parallelizing a method that loses to CP-SAT won't reach records) and sharpens the remaining autonomous lever to the STRUCTURED forms — which are substantial builds (FunSearch needs an LLM+GPU loop; automorphism-prescribed needs a symmetry-collapse encoder).
caveat: the local search is naive (O(k^2)/move, 64-sample swap); a tuned heuristic might reach CP-SAT's level but the gap to the structured record (e.g. 42) is large and not bridged by generic search.
